Details:
Conventional treatment was found to be marginally effective for the removal of TCE in one study. Based on data available from a bench-scale study, 40 percent of the contaminant was removed by coagulation with the use of alum, although it should be noted that volitilization also contributed to the removal of TCE in this study.
The study also found that 85.6 percent of the contaminant was removed with coagulation using pottasium ferrate (a combined coagulant/oxidizer).
Further, complete removal was obtained using nitrogen gas flocculation and potasium ferrate. Control is attributed to volatilization and to oxidation.
